channel.title: Cursor Blog
channel.description: Latest updates and insights from the Cursor team about AI-powered coding

item.title: Improving Composer through real-time RL
item.description: We apply online reinforcement learning to Composer, serving model checkpoints to production and using real user interactions as reward signals to ship an improved checkpoint multiple times a day.

item.title: Run cloud agents in your own infrastructure
item.description: Self-hosted cloud agents keep your code and tool execution entirely in your network.

item.title: Fast regex search: indexing text for agent tools
item.description: How we're building indexes for regular expression search so agents can find text in large monorepos without the 15-second ripgrep waits.

item.title: Introducing Composer 2
item.description: Frontier-level coding with strong CursorBench results, higher token efficiency, and a faster default variant.

item.title: Money Forward brings Cursor’s coding agents to product, design, and QA
item.description: Over 1,000 Money Forward employees now use Cursor every day.

item.title: Training Composer for longer horizons
item.description: By making self-summarization part of Composer's training, we can get training signal from trajectories much longer than the model's max context window.

item.title: Securing our codebase with autonomous agents
item.description: Cursor's security team built a fleet of security agents to find and fix vulnerabilities across a fast-changing codebase.

item.title: How we compare model quality in Cursor
item.description: We use a hybrid online-offline eval process to keep our understanding of model quality aligned with what developers actually do.

item.title: Over 30 new plugins join the Cursor Marketplace
item.description: Extend Cursor with prebuilt capabilities in our marketplace.

item.title: Build agents that run automatically
item.description: Cursor now supports automations that run based on triggers and instructions you define.
